description: Declarative source for the ai_studio refactor tree. The architecture map page is only a renderer; serve ai_studio/architecture_map/index.html and validate this JSON with ai_studio/architecture_map/validate_map.mjs.

root.title: ai_studio/
root.subtitle: refactor root
root.description: Clean refactor tree. Start with Core Harness only; promote modules from Not Refactored after review, cleanup, owner assignment, and compatibility checks.

children.title: ai_studio/README.md
children.description: AI Studio root index: describes available capabilities, task routing, migration rules, map ownership, and where to go for each kind of work.
children.role: AI Studio root capability index and router

children.title: Studio Shell
children.subtitle: single UI entry point
children.description: Shared browser shell for AI Studio surfaces: home, persistent navigation, collapse state, theme, and unified local serving. It hosts surfaces but does not own module domain logic.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Studio Shell module contract: owns the shared browser shell, defines surface vs module ownership, and lists hosted surfaces.

children.title: Home Surface
children.description: AI Studio home surface: opens the architecture map, taskboard, tree source, and validation report from one local URL.

children.title: server.mjs
children.description: Unified local server for AI Studio home, Architecture Map surface, Taskboard surface, tree.json, validation report, and Taskboard API routes.

children.title: start_site.mjs
children.description: Stable launcher for the AI Studio local site. Starts the server in the background, writes PID and logs, verifies the page, supports --open, --restart, and --port.

children.title: start_site.cmd
children.description: Small Windows wrapper for the Windows-safe AI Studio launcher.
children.role: Windows AI Studio site launch helper

children.title: start_site_windows.ps1
children.description: Windows-safe detached launcher for Codex and local browser use. It avoids managed-shell child-process cleanup, writes PID/log files, waits for HTTP readiness, and supports -Restart, -Open, and -Port.
children.role: Windows-safe AI Studio site launch helper

children.title: Shared UI Implementation
children.subtitle: css/js internals
children.description: Low-level browser files for the shared AI Studio shell. Hidden from the default graph so Studio Shell stays readable as one architectural surface.

children.title: home.css
children.description: AI Studio home-surface content styling. The shared navigation shell is owned by studio_shell.css and studio_shell.js.

children.title: studio_shell.css
children.description: Shared dark AI Studio app shell: persistent left navigation, collapsed navigation state, page content area, and responsive sidebar behavior.

children.title: studio_shell.js
children.description: Shared browser behavior for the AI Studio left sidebar: collapse and expand toggle with localStorage persistence.

children.title: Core Harness
children.subtitle: ai_studio/core_harness
children.description: Harness, agent-facing contract, AGENTS.md, root compatibility surfaces, passive profiling, and the smallest public entry points needed to start work.
Core Harness module contract: what belongs in the always-nearby harness, what is only a compatibility surface, and how Core routes into workflow, orchestration, and validation.
Repo-level harness contract loaded by agents: repository role, AI Studio direction, current-game routing, hard invariants, and context routes.
Compatibility pointer for Claude-facing harnesses; should stay generated or thin and route back to AGENTS.md.
Current-game routing file. Keeps active game context out of AGENTS.md and points to game design docs, task state, and game folder when a game is active.

children.title: Workflow
children.subtitle: task execution order
children.description: Core Harness workflow group: local process for context loading, work loop, validation handoff, and delegation checkpoint.
children.role: agent task workflow inside the harness

children.description: Canonical Workflow contract: context policy, lead-agent work loop, validation handoff, and delegation checkpoint.

children.title: Orchestration
children.subtitle: early subagent split
children.description: Child rule inside Workflow for broad read-heavy work. Keeps subagent use lightweight with Task / Scope / Return / Stop packets; it is not a product, task store, or public CLI.
children.role: workflow delegation trigger and packet shape

children.description: Short orchestration contract: when to split work, the simple packet format, lead responsibility, and boundary with Workflow and Taskboard.

children.title: Agent Surfaces
children.subtitle: generated Codex/Claude surfaces
children.description: Core Harness module for generated agent-facing compatibility surfaces: Claude skill pointers and Codex/Claude hook configs.
children.role: agent surface generation and drift checks

children.title: README.md
children.description: Agent Surfaces contract: canonical sources, generated outputs, command entrypoints, and module boundaries.

children.title: sync.mjs
children.description: Public Agent Surfaces facade that regenerates or checks all generated Codex/Claude compatibility files.

children.title: skills_sync.mjs
children.description: Generates .claude/skills thin pointers from Codex-discoverable .codex/skills entrypoints and removes stale generated pointers.

children.title: hooks_sync.mjs
children.description: Generates .codex/hooks.json and the hooks block of .claude/settings.json from one canonical hook source.

children.title: Generated Outputs
children.subtitle: Codex/Claude compatibility files
children.description: Generated compatibility files produced by Agent Surfaces. Edit the canonical source, not these outputs.

children.title: .codex/hooks.json
children.description: Generated Codex hook configuration rendered from hooks_sync.mjs.

children.title: .claude/settings.json
children.description: Generated Claude hook configuration. hooks_sync.mjs preserves Claude-only keys while replacing the hooks block.

children.title: .claude/skills/
children.description: Generated Claude skill pointer directory. Each pointer routes to the matching Codex-discoverable .codex/skills entrypoint.

children.title: Tests
children.subtitle: sync regressions
children.description: Regression tests for generated skill pointers and hook config rendering.

children.title: skills_sync.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for generated skill pointer creation, stale pointer detection, and hand-written skill preservation.

children.title: hooks_sync.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests that hook generation reproduces committed Codex/Claude config and preserves tool-specific matcher vocabularies.

children.title: Profiling
children.subtitle: passive session telemetry
children.description: Core Harness profiling group: passive hook telemetry, Codex session recovery, status reports, subagent diagnostics, and NT chat-session reflection ownership.
children.role: agent execution observability inside the harness

children.title: README.md
children.description: Profiling module contract: Agent Surfaces owns hook config; Profiling owns recorder commands, status reports, Codex recovery, and reflection skill instructions.

children.title: status.mjs
children.description: Reads per-session profile JSONL and reports command durations, slow work, repeated commands, failures, coverage gaps, and optional subagent diagnostics.

children.title: hook_record.mjs
children.description: JS hook recorder fallback for command results, Codex failure recovery, manual --recover-only import, and subagent spawn telemetry. Called by generated hooks for non-hot-path cases.

children.title: hook_record_fast.c
children.description: Native fast hook recorder source for low-overhead SessionStart, PreToolUse, and PostToolUse command telemetry.

children.title: hook_record_fast.exe
children.description: Built Windows fast hook recorder used by generated Codex and Claude hook configs.

children.title: build_hook_record_fast.mjs
children.description: Build helper for the native fast hook recorder.

children.title: profile_lib.mjs
children.description: Shared profile path, JSONL, parsing, timestamp, command, and record helpers used by profiling commands.

children.title: agent_rollup.mjs
children.description: Best-effort subagent transcript diagnostics for Claude and Codex sessions. Diagnostic only, not an acceptance gate.

children.title: NT Chat Session Reflection Skill
children.subtitle: session retrospective instructions
children.description: Canonical skill instructions for full retrospective analysis of long AI-assisted sessions using durable evidence, profiling telemetry, and chat/session context.

children.title: .codex skill entrypoint
children.description: Codex-discoverable compatibility entrypoint that points to the canonical profiling-owned skill instructions.

children.title: profiling.test.mjs
children.description: Focused profiling regression tests for hook records, Codex recovery, status reports, coverage, and subagent rollups.

children.title: Validation
children.subtitle: fast route checks
children.description: Fast Core Harness validation for agent-facing docs, stale file references, and retired command routes. Module test suites stay with their owning modules.
children.role: core harness route and reference guard

children.description: Core validation contract: fast mechanical checks only; no global pipeline validation.
Mechanical guard for stale local references, missing files, and retired commands in agent-facing Markdown.
Regression tests for the stale-reference guard and retired command detection.

children.title: Taskboard
children.subtitle: tasks/ + ai_studio/taskboard
children.description: Durable project work state: tasks, epics, context digest, and task-store validation.
Taskboard contract for task files, statuses, task lifecycle, durable context, and task CLI usage.
Detailed task-store reference for fields, lifecycle, and maintenance rules.

children.title: nt-taskboard-manager
children.description: Thin Codex skill router for Taskboard work. It activates task capture, planning, reporting, and status updates, then routes agents to the Taskboard product/API contract.
Public Taskboard CLI for listing, context digest, task create/update, and task-store validation.
Taskboard implementation library: parsing, validation, context digest, task mutation, and task-state checks used by orchestration.
Taskboard HTTP API adapter for board payloads and task/epic CRUD routes. Studio Shell mounts it but does not own the Taskboard domain contract.
Taskboard browser surface. Served by Studio Shell; Taskboard owns the surface content, not the shared server.

children.title: Surface Implementation
children.subtitle: public static files
children.description: Static browser files behind the Taskboard surface. Hidden from the default graph so the page is represented as one surface unless implementation details are needed.

children.title: index.html
children.description: Taskboard browser document hosted at /taskboard/ by Studio Shell.

children.title: style.css
children.description: Taskboard page-specific styling. Shared navigation styling comes from Studio Shell.

children.title: app.js
children.description: Taskboard browser behavior for loading, editing, and rendering tasks through the shared Taskboard API.

children.title: markdown_preview.js
children.description: Small Markdown preview helper used by the Taskboard browser surface.
Taskboard regression tests for parsing, commands, context, validation, and task lifecycle behavior.

children.title: Assets
children.subtitle: reviewed asset tools
children.description: Reviewed AI Studio asset modules: browser viewing surfaces plus the storage layer for source registries, OKF catalog reading, and derived previews.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Assets module contract: what has moved into AI Studio and which legacy asset-pipeline areas still need separate review.

children.title: Assets Storage
children.subtitle: sources, manifests, indexes, previews
children.description: Storage layer for asset source registries, editable pack manifests, legacy OKF catalog reading, generated SQLite indexes, agent search, and derived preview preparation. The browser viewer consumes this layer but does not own it.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Assets Storage module contract: source registries, pack manifests, generated SQLite indexes, preview preparation, agent search, and boundary with Asset Viewer.

children.title: search_assets.mjs
children.description: Agent-facing asset search command over generated SQLite indexes. Supports query, kind, tags, origin, license, pack, JSON output, and custom source paths.

children.title: search_assets.test.mjs
children.description: Regression test proving agent asset search uses the generated index for a pack manifest source.

children.title: Source Registry
children.subtitle: explicit asset roots
children.description: Explicit registry for game-local asset folders. Avoids guessing games by scanning the repository root.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Source Registry contract: why game asset roots are explicit and how new games register asset folders.

children.title: templates.json
children.description: Explicit registry of template asset folders shown by Asset Viewer.

children.title: templates_registry.mjs
children.description: Small helper for reading, listing, and upserting template asset source records.

children.title: templates_registry.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for default template source listing and template source upserts.

children.title: games.json
children.description: Explicit registry of game-local asset folders shown by Asset Viewer. Updated by tools/bootstrap/new_game.mjs.

children.title: games_registry.mjs
children.description: Small helper for reading, listing, and upserting game asset source records.

children.title: games_registry.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for creating, listing, normalizing, and upserting registered game asset sources.

children.title: Pack Manifest
children.subtitle: pack.json + assets.jsonl
children.description: Preferred editable canonical format for reviewed asset packs. Stores pack metadata in pack.json and one asset record per line in assets.jsonl; generated SQLite indexes consume it for fast search.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Pack Manifest contract: layout, pack.json fields, assets.jsonl rows, path boundary, and current Asset Index integration.

children.title: pack_manifest.mjs
children.description: Reads packs/<pack-id>/pack.json and assets.jsonl into normalized asset records and pack summaries for Asset Index.

children.title: pack_manifest.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for pack.json/assets.jsonl reading, inherited pack metadata, relative paths, previews, and model paths.

children.title: OKF Catalog
children.subtitle: markdown asset records
children.description: Markdown/OKF shared library reader and source-first search entrypoint. Binary files and derived previews remain in the asset storage folders referenced by catalog records.

children.title: README.md
children.description: OKF Catalog contract: catalog record reader, shared-library scan, source-first search, and legacy compatibility entrypoint.

children.title: find_assets.mjs
children.description: Reads OKF Markdown catalog records, joins pack metadata, searches the shared asset library, and exports scanLibrary for storage consumers.
children.role: OKF catalog reader and source-first search command

children.title: Asset Index
children.subtitle: sqlite query layer
children.description: Local SQLite indexes, one per asset source, built from OKF catalog records, pack manifests, or folder scans. Serves fast page, search, filter, pack, and model lookups for Asset Viewer and agent search without rescanning sources on each request.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Asset Index contract: one generated SQLite database per source, explicit rebuild/refresh, public query API, no watch mode, and boundary with OKF Catalog, Pack Manifest, folder scans, and Asset Viewer.

children.title: asset_index.mjs
children.description: Builds and queries the local asset SQLite index from OKF catalog records, pack manifests, or folder scans.
children.role: asset index API and rebuild helper

children.title: asset_index.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for index rebuild, pack summary, search, filters, facets, paging, and model lookup.

children.title: Preview Pipeline
children.subtitle: derived preview preparation
children.description: Local storage preparation for derived asset previews. Currently renders GLB thumbnails through Blender and writes previews back under previews/<asset-id>/.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Preview Pipeline contract: local preparation, Blender thumbnail generation, no watch mode, and viewer boundary.

children.title: preview_cache.mjs
children.description: Source-aware preview cache refresh used by Asset Viewer. Copies or renders only missing/stale previews and refreshes the selected source index.

children.title: render_library_previews.mjs
children.description: Selects shared-library model assets and runs Blender thumbnail rendering in resumable chunks.

children.title: render_thumbs.py
children.description: Blender script that renders one isometric thumbnail per GLB using the shared studio HDR.

children.title: make_studio_hdr.py
children.description: Rebuilds the shared studio HDR used by Blender thumbnails and browser model-viewer lighting.

children.title: studio_env.hdr
children.description: Shared studio environment used by generated model thumbnails and live 3D model previews.

children.title: preview_cache.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for missing, cached, and stale source-aware preview refresh.

children.title: Asset Viewer
children.subtitle: library and review gallery
children.description: Browser-facing asset gallery for library browsing, game-asset review, pick manifests, 3D model inspection, promotion, and reuse pulls.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Asset Viewer module contract: modes, commands, ownership boundaries, and why the gallery server remains separate.

children.title: Gallery Entry
children.subtitle: source picker inside gallery
children.description: Asset Viewer entry hosted at /asset_viewer/. The gallery itself lists available sources and switches between all assets, template assets, and the current game.

children.title: index.html
children.description: Asset Viewer browser entry. It loads the existing gallery UI and lets the user choose the displayed asset source from inside the gallery header.

children.title: api.mjs
children.description: Studio Shell API adapter for listing asset sources, opening the selected source in the gallery, rebuilding/querying the asset index, and resolving gallery media.

children.title: build_review.mjs
children.description: Generates static asset gallery outputs for shared-library browsing, game-asset review, or direct asset-folder scans.

children.title: serve_gallery.mjs
children.description: Serves a generated asset gallery and maps a large external asset library at /lib/ so library browsing can avoid copying every media file.

children.title: promote.mjs
children.description: Promotes selected assets from a review manifest into the shared library with catalog metadata, license records, and integrity data.

children.title: pull.mjs
children.description: Copies selected shared-library assets into a game-local asset tree while preserving source_id linkage and restricted-asset routing.

children.title: record_gallery.mjs
children.description: Records a browser session of the asset gallery for review or presentation evidence.

children.title: Surface Implementation
children.subtitle: viewer js/css
children.description: Static browser files copied into generated gallery outputs. Hidden from the default graph so Asset Viewer remains readable as one surface.

children.title: viewer.js
children.description: Vanilla browser SPA for filters, pack views, asset modal, picking, and model-viewer integration.

children.title: viewer.css
children.description: Asset Viewer page styling for gallery cards, filters, sheets, and model previews.

children.title: Tests
children.subtitle: asset viewer regressions
children.description: Regression tests for Asset Viewer generation and promotion behavior.

children.title: build_review.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for asset kind classification, origin detection, escaping, and gallery HTML rendering.

children.title: promote.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for promoting picked assets into the shared library with catalog and license records.

children.title: Architecture Map
children.subtitle: tree renderer and map validation
children.description: Self-map for this AI Studio refactor tree: live HTML renderer, declarative tree data, and validation report for unmapped or stale files.

children.title: tree.json
children.description: Declarative source for the working ai_studio tree: hierarchy, node ids, manual labels, roles, tags, and explicit child order.
children.role: declarative source of the ai_studio tree

children.title: README.md
children.description: Architecture Map module contract: tree.json is architecture truth, index.html only renders, validate_map.mjs only audits.

children.title: Architecture Map Surface
children.description: Browser surface and renderer for tree.json and validation-report.json. It is hosted by Studio Shell but owned by Architecture Map.

children.title: validate_map.mjs
children.description: Scans known AI Studio and legacy pipeline paths and writes validation-report.json with unmapped, missing, duplicate, and underspecified files.
children.role: architecture map scanner and report writer

children.title: validation-report.json
children.description: Generated report displayed by the map page so newly created or unmapped files are visible during refactoring.

children.title: validate_map.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for missing, duplicate, unmapped, and mapped-directory validation behavior.

children.title: Quality
children.subtitle: small rule set
children.description: Reviewed quality module: numbered markdown checks with 001 as the base rule, lightweight quality evidence, and rule-usage profiling.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Quality module entrypoint: rule selection, group boundaries, numbered checks, evidence recording, and profiling.

children.title: nt-quality-checks
children.description: Codex-discoverable quality-check skill that routes agents through AI Studio quality groups, matching rule files, outcome logging, and usage profiling.

children.title: Rule Groups
children.subtitle: numbered checks
children.description: Grouped quality rules. Read each matching group README, use the 001 rule when it matches, then only the higher-numbered checks needed for the task.

children.title: Player Clarity Rules
children.subtitle: QCLR
children.description: Rules for UI/UX, HUD, scene clarity, sprite readability/state clarity, feedback, interactive elements, responsive viewports, virtual controls, and player-facing presentation.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Player Clarity rule group router for screen state, responsive viewports, and virtual controls.

children.title: QCLR_001 Player Clarity
children.description: Check basic player-facing clarity: visible output, readable important information, visible actions and objects, and understandable next action.

children.title: QCLR_002 Responsive Viewports
children.description: Check 4:3, 16:9, and tall-phone viewport/orientation layouts for cropped, hidden, overlapped, or misplaced player-facing UI.

children.title: QCLR_003 Virtual Controls
children.description: Check mobile/touch controls, virtual buttons, joystick zones, and control hints for visibility, reachability, overlap, and clarity.

children.title: Art Rules
children.subtitle: QART
children.description: Rules for player-facing visuals, generated art, screenshots, visual targets, and avoiding debug/placeholder defaults.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Art rule group router for player-facing visuals, generated art, screenshots, visual targets, and style/appearance fit.

children.title: QART_001 Closest Practical Visual
children.description: Check that player-facing visuals do not default to debug shapes, placeholder art, or temporary overlays when a better visual can be created, sourced, or adapted.

children.title: GDD Rules
children.subtitle: QGDD
children.description: Rules for design source packages, source order, file roles, contradictions, requirements, acceptance criteria, and implementation-facing specs.

children.title: README.md
children.description: GDD rule group router for design source packages, source order, file roles, cross-file contradictions, and acceptance proof.

children.title: QGDD_001 Design Source Readiness
children.description: Check GDD/spec/table/data/handoff packages as current source of truth: entrypoint, file roles, decisions, open questions, contradictions, and proof.

children.title: Game Design Rules
children.subtitle: QDES
children.description: Rules for playable loops, player motivation, economy, progression, feature fit, rewards, challenge, first slices, and design data.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Game design rule group router for gameplay loops, motivation, economy, progression, rewards, challenge, first slices, and design data.

children.title: QDES_001 Playable Loop
children.description: Check gameplay model, player action, system response, visible progress/reward, repeat reason, next hook, dead/confusing states, and feature fit.

children.title: Technical Rules
children.subtitle: QTECH
children.description: Rules for code, scripts, generated data, runtime behavior, build/run proof, input, state, save/load, packaging, and automation.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Technical rule group router for QTECH_001 behavior evidence.

children.title: QTECH_001 Behavior Evidence
children.description: Check changed technical claims with the narrowest useful proof: test, command, parser/schema, build/pack, launch/smoke, scenario, log, or generated-output check.

children.title: Asset Rules
children.subtitle: QASSET
children.description: Rules for sourced, generated, and runtime assets; provenance; licenses; manifests; publishability; runtime-ready formats; and material readiness.

children.title: README.md
children.description: Asset rule group router for added/changed assets, project-use, publishability, runtime-ready, player-facing, and material-dependent claims.

children.title: QASSET_001 Asset Readiness
children.description: Check accepted/project assets for source/provenance/origin, committed integrity, license/publish routing, project-local paths, runtime format/path, and load/render proof.

children.title: QASSET_002 Material Readiness
children.description: Check material-dependent assets for textures, UVs, colors, material maps, material assignment, and stage-appropriate material proof.

children.title: profile.mjs
children.description: Scans task logs for Quality rule outcome lines and summarizes rule usage, pass, block, review, and skip counts.

children.title: profile.test.mjs
children.description: Regression tests for quality rule usage profiling from task logs.

children.title: Not Refactored
children.subtitle: current scattered pipeline
children.description: Everything that exists today but has not yet been reviewed, cleaned, assigned an owner, and moved into the main ai_studio tree.

children.title: Legacy Pipeline Routes
children.subtitle: known old docs/tools
children.description: Known pre-refactor pipeline docs and tools. They are intentionally visible as not-refactored until each one is reviewed, moved to an owning module, or deleted.

children.title: tools/README.md
children.description: Legacy tools route map. Keep visible until tool ownership is split across AI Studio modules.

children.title: profiling-reuse.md
children.description: Legacy profiling, reuse, closeout, and export reference awaiting ownership review.

children.title: new_game.mjs
children.description: Legacy game bootstrap command. It copies template/ into a new game folder and now registers the game's asset root in AI Studio.

children.title: new_game.test.mjs
children.description: Regression test proving legacy game bootstrap registers game asset roots without touching the real repository.

children.title: TEMPLATE.md
children.description: Legacy portable export template contract awaiting export-module ownership review.
